data.title: Norsk Titanium Grants New Long-Term Incentive Shares to Key Executives
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Norsk Titanium AS ( (DE:84F) ) just unveiled an update.

Norsk Titanium AS has issued a new grant of 23,993,998 restricted share units and performance shares under its long-term incentive programme, equivalent to 2.07% of its outstanding share capital. The move underscores the company’s use of equity-based rewards to retain key staff and align management interests with shareholders as it pursues growth in metal 3D printing markets.

The restricted share units will vest over three years on a time-based schedule, while the performance shares will vest after 2028 depending on financial and operational targets set by the board. Significant allocations were made to primary insiders, highlighting the board’s focus on incentivising top leadership, which may support long-term execution of Norsk Titanium’s strategy in aerospace, defence and industrial applications.

More about Norsk Titanium AS

Norsk Titanium AS is a global leader in metal 3D printing, specialising in its proprietary Rapid Plasma Deposition (RPD) technology to produce value-added metal parts. With 700 MT of production capacity, the company targets commercial aerospace, defence and industrial markets, offering customers cost-efficient components that reduce raw material use, energy consumption and environmental impact compared with traditional metal forming.

Average Trading Volume: 4,401,835

Current Market Cap: NOK1.19B

data.title: Juventus Secures Long-Term Signing of Jeff Ekhator from Genoa
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Juventus Football Club Spa ( (IT:JUVE) ) has provided an update.

Juventus has agreed with Genoa CFC to permanently acquire the registration rights of midfielder Jeff Osayuki Ekhator for €16 million, payable over three financial years, plus €0.4 million in additional charges. The deal also includes performance-related bonuses up to €2 million and a contract with the player running until 30 June 2031, underscoring Juventus’s strategy of long-term squad investment and potential value creation in the transfer market.

The structure of the transaction, with staged payments and conditional bonuses, reflects a measured approach to financial planning while strengthening the team’s sporting assets. By locking in Ekhator on a five-year deal, Juventus signals confidence in his contribution to future competitive performance, which could impact both on-field results and the club’s balance sheet through amortized transfer costs and potential resale value.

The most recent analyst rating on (IT:JUVE) stock is a Hold
with a EUR2.35 price target.

see the IT:JUVE Stock Forecast page.

More about Juventus Football Club Spa

Juventus Football Club S.p.A. is a leading Italian professional football club competing in Serie A and European competitions, generating revenue primarily from match operations, broadcasting rights, sponsorships, merchandising, and player trading within the global football industry.

The latest announcement is out from Elementis ( (GB:ELM) ).

Elementis plc has corrected a previous disclosure regarding its share capital and total voting rights, updating investors on the accurate figures as of 30 June 2026. The company now reports an issued share capital of 570,998,879 ordinary shares, with 212,417 shares held in treasury, resulting in 570,786,462 voting rights.

The clarification ensures shareholders and market participants have the correct denominator for calculating their holdings under Financial Conduct Authority transparency rules. By promptly rectifying the error in its earlier announcement, Elementis reinforces compliance with disclosure regulations and provides clearer visibility on its capital structure for investors and other stakeholders.

The latest announcement is out from Lloyds Banking ( (GB:LLOY) ).

Lloyds Banking Group has repurchased 5,000,000 of its ordinary shares on 1 July 2026 through Goldman Sachs International as part of its ongoing share buyback programme. The shares were bought at prices between 110.5p and 112.1p, with a volume-weighted average price of 111.3618p, and the bank plans to cancel the repurchased stock, effectively reducing its share count.

The transaction continues a previously announced capital return strategy, signalling management’s confidence in the group’s capital position and future earnings capacity. By shrinking its share base, Lloyds is likely to enhance earnings per share over time, which may support shareholder value and underscores the bank’s disciplined approach to capital management in a competitive U.K. banking market.

Sanderson Design Group PLC ( (GB:SDG) ) just unveiled an update.

Sanderson Design Group PLC, a luxury interior furnishings specialist with brands including Zoffany, Sanderson, Morris & Co., Harlequin, Clarke & Clarke and Scion, combines in-house UK manufacturing of wallpapers and fabrics with global distribution and design licensing. Its products are sold worldwide through showrooms in London, New York and Chicago, underpinning its positioning in the premium interiors market.

At its latest Annual General Meeting, shareholders of Sanderson Design Group approved all resolutions except one relating to an additional 10% share issuance authority for acquisitions or capital investment. While the board acknowledged notable levels of opposition to that measure and to certain remuneration and director re-election votes, it stressed its commitment to ongoing engagement with shareholders, signalling governance and capital allocation remain key areas of investor focus.

Sanderson Design Group PLC is a UK-based luxury interior furnishings company that designs, manufactures and markets wallpapers, fabrics and paints, and licenses its designs for products such as bed and bath collections, rugs, blinds and tableware. data.title: Zumtobel scraps dividend after profit hit from tax impairments
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

An announcement from Zumtobel ( (GB:0MJH) ) is now available.

Zumtobel Group AG has proposed suspending its dividend for the 2025/26 financial year, marking a break from its established payout policy as the company contends with weaker results. Group revenue declined by 5.2% to EUR 1,040.4 million, while adjusted EBIT slipped to EUR 42.4 million, with profitability further pressured by special effects that pushed EBIT margin down to 2.2% and profit before tax to EUR 10.7 million.

Net profit fell sharply to around EUR 1 million from EUR 15.5 million a year earlier, largely due to substantially higher income taxes driven by impairments of deferred tax assets in the USA, the United Kingdom and Austria, along with one-off effects. Against this backdrop, management has opted to conserve cash by omitting a dividend, while signaling an expectation to return to its usual dividend policy in the following financial year, a move of particular relevance for income-focused shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:0MJH) stock is a Hold
with a EUR4.50 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Zumtobel stock,
see the GB:0MJH Stock Forecast page.

More about Zumtobel

Zumtobel Group AG is an Austria-based lighting company that develops and manufactures professional lighting solutions and related components. The group focuses on commercial, industrial and architectural applications, and is listed on the Vienna Stock Exchange, giving it exposure to European capital markets and institutional investors.

Helios Towers will release its half-year 2026 results for the period to 30 June on 30 July and is organizing an in-person presentation for analysts and institutional investors in London, alongside a webcast and conference call with replay access via its website. Management plans to pair the results briefing with a strategic deep-dive on its multi-decade growth runway and the structural demand for telecoms infrastructure in its African and Middle Eastern markets, signaling continued emphasis on long-term value creation and reinforcing its profile through participation in several major industry conferences later in the year.

The company’s planned discussion of long-term growth prospects aims to highlight how its tower assets and colocation model position it to benefit from sustained expansion in mobile services and digital connectivity across its footprint. data.title: Viaplay to Sell Dutch Operations to Videoland for EUR 142 Million
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Viaplay ( ($SE:VPLAY.B) ) has issued an update.

Viaplay Group has agreed to sell its Dutch streaming and broadcasting operations to Videoland, owned by DPG Media, for EUR 142 million on a cash- and debt-free basis, pending regulatory and lender approvals. The divestment aligns with Viaplay’s strategy to refocus on its core Nordic markets, where it sees the greatest scale and synergies.

Management said the proceeds will be used to accelerate deleveraging and strengthen the company’s financial profile, following last year’s acquisition of the remaining 50% of Allente and subsequent refinancing. The Dutch unit generated EUR 149 million in net sales in 2025, and while the sale will trigger transaction and restructuring costs booked as items affecting comparability, Viaplay expects no change to its 2026 full-year financial targets or long-term ambitions.

The most recent analyst rating on ($SE:VPLAY.B) stock is a Hold
with a SEK1.45 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Viaplay stock,
see the SE:VPLAY.B Stock Forecast page.

More about Viaplay

Viaplay Group is the Nordic region’s leading entertainment provider, operating the Viaplay streaming service across the Nordics and in the Netherlands, alongside TV channels and commercial radio stations in Norway and Sweden. The company also owns Allente, a major Nordic TV and broadband provider, and focuses on locally relevant content spanning premium live sports, films, series and music, with shares listed on Nasdaq Stockholm.

Average Trading Volume: 14,149,452

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.title: AFT Pharmaceuticals Advances Pediatric IV Pain Study With Combogesic®
data.description:
AFT Pharmaceuticals Ltd. (AU:AFP) announced an update on their ongoing clinical study.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

AFT Pharmaceuticals Ltd. (AFP) is preparing a Phase 3 study titled “An Open Label, Pharmacokinetic and Safety Study of Combogesic® IV in Pediatric Patients With Acute Pain.” The trial will track how children and teens process the medicine and how safe it is, aiming to support wider use of the product in hospital pain care.

The treatment under review is Combogesic® IV, an intravenous mix of ibuprofen and acetaminophen. It is designed to provide fast, balanced pain relief for children after surgery or injury, while helping reduce the need for opioid medicines.

The study will use a simple, single‑group design, with all enrolled patients receiving Combogesic® IV. There is no placebo and no blinding, making it easier to focus on safety, drug levels in the blood, and real‑world pain control outcomes.

The trial is not yet recruiting, with first submission recorded on October 30, 2025. The most recent update on June 29, 2026 signals that planning remains active, and key completion dates will guide when AFP can seek regulatory decisions and expand marketing.

For investors, this pediatric study extends AFP’s pain portfolio into a key hospital niche, which could support future revenue and valuation if results are positive. Competing IV pain options exist, but a well‑tolerated combo that lowers opioid use may attract demand and strengthen AFP’s position in the acute care market.

The Combogesic® IV pediatric study is currently in the setup phase with ongoing updates, and further details are available on the ClinicalTrials portal.

To learn more about AU:AFP’s potential, visit the AFT Pharmaceuticals Ltd. drug pipeline page.
